commit_message:
Fix crash when scrolling through cited by in Citation relations (#12877)

* Fix crash when scrolling through cited by in Citation relations

Uses Webview instead of Label and TextFlowLimited
when trying to display right to left text
(like persian or arabic) in BibEntryView. This resolves the
issue caused by a javafx bug that causes an exception when
rendering wrapped RTL text.
Fixes #12410

* fix: Apply OpenRewrite auto-fixes

* fix: Revert unintended submodule changes to csl-styles and csl-locales

* fix: Replaces WebView with scrollable Text/Label nodes

1. Open the citation relations tab in entry editor

issue_title: @string removed while restoring backups
issue_body:
### JabRef version

5.15 (latest release)

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

OpenSuse Tumbleweed

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Open a bib file with `@string` entries
2. Make some change without saving
3. Have a look at the automatic backup (`*.bak`) file
4. Observe how the `@string` entry isn't included there


### Appendix

I had suffered several times from having all my `@string`s removed from bib files. Today, it happened again and I noted that it was after a recovery from an automatic backup.

In fact, checking the `*.bak` files jabref writes, it seems that they do not include `@string` entries. Hence, these are lost on restorage.

I'd rate this as a rather severe (dataloss) bug.

commit_message:
[Junie]: Filter specific ClassCastException warnings in JavaFX logs (#14666)

* Filter specific ClassCastException warnings in JavaFX logs
 ### What I changed
- Implemented a precise filter for the noisy JavaFX CSS warnings that appear when opening the entry editor.
- Added a JUL filter that drops only those warnings which come from stylesheet processing (JavaFX’s Modena theme or JabRef’s Base.css) and match “Could not resolve …” or “while converting value …” patterns.
- Wired the filter into JabRef’s startup before the SLF4J ↔ tinylog bridge is installed, so the messages never reach tinylog, the console, or the GUI event log.
- Added a small unit test to ensure the filter blocks the targeted messages and allows unrelated warnings through.

### Technical details
- New class: `org.jabref.gui.logging.JavaFxCssLogFilter` (implements `java.util.logging.Filter`).
  - Filters only `Level.WARNING` records whose message both:
    - Mentions stylesheet processing and is tied to either `modena.bss` (JavaFX) or `/org/jabref/gui/Base.css` (JabRef), and
    - Contains either “Could not resolve '…' while resolving lookups …” or “while converting value for '…' …”.
  - Also suppresses attached `ClassCastException`/`IllegalArgumentException` thrown during stylesheet parsing for those sources.
- Hookup: In `Launcher.initLogging()`, before `SLF4JBridgeHandler.install()`:
  - `Logger.getLogger("").setFilter(new JavaFxCssLogFilter());`
- Tests: `JavaFxCssLogFilterTest` validates that CSS conversion/lookup warnings (examples from the issue) are filtered and unrelated warnings pass.

### Why this approach
- The issue arises from JavaFX CSS (cf. JDK-8268657). Filtering at the JUL source avoids over-filtering by class name alone and keeps other `sun.util.*` logs intact.
- It keeps tinylog configuration untouched and avoids writer-specific duplication: both console output and file/event logs are silenced for these specific warnings.
- The match is conservative (requires references to Modena or Base.css and specific message phrases), reducing the risk of hiding useful logs.

### Validation
- `:jabgui:test --tests org.jabref.gui.logging.JavaFxCssLogFilterTest` passes locally.
- The new filter is isolated to GUI startup and does not affect other modules.

### Follow-ups
- If JavaFX fixes the root cause, this filter can remain harmlessly (it matches nothing) or be removed.
- If more CSS warning variants appear, extend the pattern checks in `JavaFxCssLogFilter` accordingly.

* fix logger install

* simplify

### **Problem Description  **

- Update the broken link with the correct URL: `[correct-link-here]`.
- If possible, verify that the correct link works properly before updating.
- A pull request (PR) can be submitted to update the `Contributing.md` file with the correct link.

issue_title: Abbreviation of a list of entries cause two uncaught exceptions
issue_body:
### JabRef version

Latest development branch build (please note build date below)

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

Windows 11

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Create a new library.
2. Use "Web Search" to search for something (for example, “SW”).
3. Select all resulting entries and import them into your library.
4. Select all the imported entries
5. From the "Quality" tab, abbreviate the journal names to "dotless." If the error does not appear, abbreviate them to "shortUnique" and repeat these steps a couple of times. The exceptions do not occur consistently.

**Explanation:** 
I traced the code and found that the issue occurs because a `CompoundEdit` object, which is not thread-safe, is accessed concurrently. In the `AbbreviationAction` class, the abbreviate method creates multiple Callable tasks that use NamedCompound instance. When I changed the code to iterate over the entries in a regular loop (instead of collecting tasks in threads) the errors no longer occurred.

The buttons "Search" and "Add group" need to be disabled if there is no library opened. If possible, also the selection of the search provider and the search text box should be disabled.

issue_title: Fix "Copy to" preferences
issue_body:
### Sub-issue 1:
1. Start JabRef
2. Open a library X (and save it)
3. Open another library Y (and save it)
4. Have (one or more) entries in the library X with cross-references (and save). Example: 

    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e3b08fd-693a-4d3f-8276-a2b4c86be286)
4. Right click the entry/entries with cross references.
5. Select "Copy to" -> "Y"
6. Tick "Do not ask again" and then select "Include":
      
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/23499016-0f08-4d3e-be25-5cc627d1a0e0)
7. Restart JabRef
8. Open the two saved libraries X and Y (if not open already)
9. Repeat steps 5-6.
10. You will observe that the dialog (in step 7) appears again even though we ticked "Do not ask again". (Expected: It respects last saved preferences, i.e., does not appear).

#### Related PRs:
- https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12374
- https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12503

### Sub-issue 2:
1. Start JabRef
2. Go to File -> Preferences
3. In "General" -> "User Interface", observe the state of the option "Ask whether to include cross-references when copying to another library".
    
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/185c2c9a-4888-4e89-a378-f609dac924bd)

4. Change its state (in this case, it is checked, so we un-check it)
5. Click 'Save"
6. Restart JabRef
7. Repeat steps 1-3. You will find that the box (in step 3) is checked again (updated state of preferences was not saved). (Expected: Last saved state of the preference persists across sessions).

#### Related PR:
- https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12526

### Test for completion (for both sub-issues)
1. The state of the preferences are saved across sessions. 
2. (After 1 is ensured) - Double-check that the functionality (not just saved state) of the respective options works as expected too after the restart.

Screen recordings are preferable for easy verification.

issue_title: Paste of URL with .pdf into empty entry should get the PDF and import it
issue_body:
Filed in issue-melting pot as https://github.com/JabRef/jabref-issue-melting-pot/issues/201

### JabRef version

JabRef 6.0alpha

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

_No response_

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

To reproduce:

1. Copy `https://www.cncf.io/wp-content/uploads/2020/08/OAM-Webinar-V2.pdf` in the clipboard
2. Open JabRef
3. Go to the main table
4. Press <kbd>Ctrl</kbd>+<kbd>V</kbd>

Currently:

![image](https://github.com/JabRef/jabref-issue-melting-pot/assets/1366654/7b3057ef-c68b-4ddd-ab4e-047414833bb1)

Expected:

PDF is downloaded, parsed, etc.

---

Task: Add proper handling.

The code should be related to `org.jabref.gui.LibraryTab#handleNonBibTeXStringData`. Try to debug whats happening when pasting an URL ending with `pdf`. Add handling to that.

Hint: Adapt `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.ImportHandler#handleStringData`. Check if data is a URL (maybe using org.jabref.gui.fieldeditors.URLUtil#isURL) and ends with `pdf`. If yes, then:

1. Download PDF into the directory `databaseContext.getFirstExistingFileDir(filePreferences)`. (See org.jabref.gui.linkedfile.DownloadLinkedFileAction#execute for other uses).
2. Import the file using `org.jabref.logic.importer.fileformat.PdfMergeMetadataImporter`.

---

Questions:

- Should first a LinkedFile be generated, then download, then parsing, then merge of the two entries? -> Probably not
- It is similar to org.jabref.gui.maintable.MainTable#handleOnDragDroppedTableView. Maybe, we can just call `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.ImportHandler#importFilesInBackground`. This makes it similar to `org.jabref.gui.externalfiles.UnlinkedFilesDialogViewModel#startImport`.

issue_title: CSL citations in LibreOffice do not respect curly braces at author
issue_body:
### JabRef version

6.0-alpha

### Operating system

Windows

### Details on version and operating system

Windows 11

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

1. Start JabRef
2. Open a library
3. Add the following entry (copy paste to main table):
```
@Online{,
  author = {{The PGF/TikZ Team}},
  date   = {2023-01-16},
  title  = {pgf – Create PostScript and PDF graphics in TeX},
  url    = {https://ctan.org/pkg/pgf},
}
```
4. Connect to a running LibreOffice document instance using the [OO/LO panel](https://docs.jabref.org/cite/openofficeintegration#using-the-openoffice-libreoffice-interface).
5. Click on "Select style" in the panel
6. Select "IEEE" from the "CSL Styles" tab and press OK.
7. Select the entry
8. Press "Cite" to cite it in the document
9. Generate bibliography

Expected bibliography entry author:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c6561a07-5cd7-4a1d-b76d-ca9f58e4720d)

Actual:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/28275b58-79fb-4739-8a33-393361675024)

Issue: When the author name is enclosed by an extra pair of curly braces, it should be treated as it is without re-formatting, used in the case of "corporate authors". For more elaboration, see https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12930#issuecomment-2814998508.

Refs. https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/issues/12930
### Appendix

issue_title: Citation relations tab doesn't work properly!
issue_body:
### JabRef version

 **JabRef 6.0-alpha.493--2025-04-18--224290a**

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

Ubuntu 24.04 (64 bit)

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [x] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [x] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour



Citation relations are a very useful feature of JabRef, But very often this function does not work correctly.

To make sure of this, you need to take the following steps:

1. For example create the biblatex entry like this:

      ```biblatex
      @Article{Raftari_2015_Self_V_118_N_20_EN,
        author       = {Raftari, B. and Budko, N. V. and Vuik, C.},
        date         = {2015},
        doi          = {10.1063/1.4936201},
        file         = {:2015_Self-consistent_drift-diffusion-reaction_model_[SDMFTEBIWDS]_V_118_N_20_(B_Raftari).pdf:PDF},
        groups       = {eBook, Charging Models, SiO2, Al2O3, GR},
        journal      = {Journal of Applied Physics},
        journaltitle = {Journal of Applied Physics},
        language     = {english},
        number       = {20},
        pages        = {204101},
        status       = {checked},
        title        = {Self-consistent drift-diffusion-reaction model for the electron beam interaction with dielectric samples},
        volume       = {118},
        xmp_metadata = {+},
        availability = {on},
        priority     = {prio2},
        publisher    = {AIP Publishing},
        readstatus   = {skimmed},
      }
      ```

2. In an open Entry, open the **Citation relations** and after download the **Cited by** tab and scroll down to **Numerical simulation of electron beam-induced dielectric charging using advanced computational scheme for solving semilinear reaction-diffusion equation **. After that, add selected entry to the database.

   Next we see that the bib record was added with the **wrong Entry type Misc**, but the original type is InProceedings. Of course this is not the main problem!

3. Reload the JabRef or the entry (Raftari_2015_Self_V_118_N_20_EN) to see the changes in the Citation relations -> Cited By. Now we see that **Pavelchuk's** article that quotes **Raftari** has been added to our library and is highlighted in green. It works correctly!

4. As an example, you just need to change the bookmark type from **Misk** (for just added Entry of the **Pavelchuk**) for example to a book or article and open the **Raftari** Entry again.  Looking through the Citation relations -> Cided By for the **Raftari** Entry, we see that **Pavelchuk's** article has disappeared from those added to the library, although it is actually present in the library.

This is far from the only example when the program does not see already added elements. Especially if they were added not through the Cited By utility but by citation on the articles's website.

Often, simply changing lowercase characters to uppercase in the DOI will produce these results.

issue_title: `FileUtil::relativize` doesn't take symlinks into account
issue_body:
### JabRef version

5.15 (latest release)

### Operating system

GNU / Linux

### Details on version and operating system

KDE Neon - based on Ubuntu 24.04

### Checked with the latest development build (copy version output from About dialog)

- [ ] I made a backup of my libraries before testing the latest development version.
- [ ] I have tested the latest development version and the problem persists

### Steps to reproduce the behaviour

Look at
https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/blob/af362eaa31f84c14f76e83ac4e9a9327d5cecd20/src/main/java/org/jabref/logic/util/io/FileUtil.java#L261-L263



### Appendix

The effect of this is (among other things?) that when manually linking a PDF file to a bib entry, which is located in the main file directory, it can still end up being added by its full path.

This is because the configured main file directory and/or the path to the selected PDF file can contain symbolic links which make the paths appear different (which causes `FileUtils::relativize` to fail) even though they are not.

It seems that `Path::toRealPath` from Java's standard library could be used for obtaining a canonical path representation that has symlinks resolved.

issue_title: Directory check should respect relative paths
issue_body:
Setting:

1. `C:\TEMP\JabRef\test\demo` contains `demo.bib` and sub directory `lib`\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c1199939-533b-47c5-8dd7-d58c4b596da3)
2. In library properties, the check says, the path `lib` does not exist\
    ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a7039bff-3172-43ab-b1be-1c49f00ba732)


The path exists.

One needs to `resolve` (from the `Path` object) of the path of the `bib` file to check.

See also `org.jabref.model.database.BibDatabaseContext#getFileDirectories`. The method cannot be directly used, because it call `getFileDirectoryPath`, which makes the path absolute.

(Follow-up to https://github.com/JabRef/jabref/pull/12867)
